description: Install dependencies and set up the base Next.js app shell with layout, navigation, and role switching
steps: Create app/layout.tsx with root HTML structure, metadata (title: KALOVA Campaign Promo), import globals.css
Create app/page.tsx as dashboard home showing summary cards (total products, campaigns, pending tasks)

Create components/Navbar.tsx with: brand name KALOVA, navigation links (Dashboard, Form Input, Campaign Board, Checklist, Timeline), and a role switcher dropdown with 4 roles (SPV, Admin, Desainer, Marketing)
Create components/RoleContext.tsx - a React context provider for current role state (default: SPV), export useRole hook
Wire RoleContext provider in layout.tsx wrapping children
Create app/api/entries/route.ts - GET returns entries.json, POST appends new entry and saves
Create app/api/options/route.ts - GET returns options.json, PUT updates a specific option category
Create app/api/checklists/route.ts - GET returns checklists.json, PUT updates checklist state for an entry
All API routes use fs.readFileSync/writeFileSync with path.join(process.cwd(), 'data', 'filename.json')
acceptance_criteria: npm run build completes without errors
Layout renders with navbar, role switcher, and navigation links
All API routes return correct JSON data
Role context switches between SPV, Admin, Desainer, Marketing
